question.text: Under Regulation 6 of SEBI SAST 2011, what is the mandatory waiting period after completing a voluntary open offer before the acquirer can make further acquisitions (except through another voluntary or competing offer)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** After completing a voluntary open offer under Regulation 6, the acquirer cannot make further acquisitions for 6 months, except through another voluntary offer or a competing offer. This cooling-off period prevents market manipulation. **The Trap:** 52 weeks (approximately 12 months) is the look-back period for disentitlement — if shares were acquired in the preceding 52 weeks without triggering an open offer, the acquirer cannot make a voluntary offer. This is different from the 6-month post-completion restriction. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX frequently tests time-based restrictions in SAST. The 52-week and 6-month periods serve different regulatory purposes. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Plan acquisition timelines carefully — the 6-month lock-in after a voluntary offer means the acquirer loses market flexibility. Factor this into the acquisition strategy from the outset.
